document.source_title: Follow-up message by the WHO Director-General to the people of Tenerife regarding the hantavirus response
document.author: Tedros
document.summary: A follow-up message from the WHO Director-General reflecting on the successful response to the hantavirus outbreak in Tenerife, acknowledging the efforts of local authorities and health workers, and expressing condolences for the lives lost.

claims.claim_text: Three people died in connection with the outbreak on the Hondius.
claims.evidence_text: Three people died in connection with the outbreak on the Hondius.

claims.claim_text: The Spanish Government and the World Health Organization fulfilled their promises.
claims.evidence_text: everything that your Spanish Government and the World Health Organization promised came to pass, exactly as described.

entities.name: World Health Organization
entities.description: The specialized agency of the United Nations responsible for international public health.
entities.evidence_text: On behalf of the World Health Organization, on behalf of the passengers now home.

entities.name: Spanish Government
entities.evidence_text: The government of Prime Minister Sánchez honoured its obligations under international law.

entities.name: Tedros
entities.description: Director-General of the World Health Organization.

events.event: Last passengers disembarked from MV Hondius
events.evidence_text: Last Monday, I stood at the port of Granadilla de Abona and watched the last of the passengers from the MV Hondius board the vehicles.
Greetings from Geneva. It is Tedros again.

metrics.name: Number of passengers disembarked
metrics.evidence_text: More than 120 people from 23 countries have safely disembarked.

metrics.evidence_text: More than 120 people from 23 countries have safely disembarked.

geolocation.name: Tenerife
geolocation.evidence_text: I want you to know what that means to the world. You may never meet the passengers and crew who transited your port.
